title: Sourcegraph Cody Cheat Sheet
description: A practical guide for engineers using Sourcegraph Cody for code assistance and AI integration.

commands.description: Use GPT-4o model with streaming output for real-time feedback.
commands.scenario: When you need immediate feedback while coding in a collaborative environment.

commands.description: Set low temperature for deterministic and consistent outputs.
commands.scenario: When building a code review bot that needs deterministic output.

commands.command: prompt: "Explain the function of this code."
commands.description: Request a detailed explanation of a code snippet.
commands.scenario: When you need to understand unfamiliar code quickly.

commands.description: Limit the response length to 150 tokens to control output size.
commands.scenario: When you need concise answers to fit within UI constraints.

commands.command: "Think step by step. Then verify your answer."
commands.description: Encourage the model to reason through problems methodically.
Extend context length to handle larger codebases.
commands.scenario: When working with extensive code repositories.

commands.description: Request automated generation of test cases for a given function.
commands.scenario: When you need to quickly create tests for new code.

commands.description: Enable verbose mode for detailed logging and debugging.
commands.scenario: When troubleshooting issues with Cody's responses.

commands.description: Ask Cody to refactor code for better performance.
commands.scenario: When optimizing code for speed and efficiency.

commands.description: Get a high-level summary of a codebase's functionality.
commands.scenario: When onboarding new developers to a project.

commands.description: Disable caching to ensure fresh outputs every time.
commands.scenario: When working with frequently changing codebases.

commands.description: Request Cody to find security issues in the code.
commands.scenario: When conducting a security audit of your codebase.

commands.description: Set the API key for authenticating requests to Cody.
commands.scenario: When configuring Cody for the first time in your environment.

commands.description: Explicitly include specific files in the context window to improve accuracy for cross-file logic without indexing the entire repo.

commands.command: prompt: "Using the patterns found in @file:example.ts, implement @file:new_feature.ts"
commands.description: Use @-mentions to provide few-shot examples, forcing Cody to follow existing architectural patterns and coding styles.
Override the default persona to enforce strict constraints, such as 'Always use functional programming patterns' or 'Never use external libraries'.

commands.command: prompt: "Compare the implementation of [Function A] with [Function B] and highlight edge case discrepancies."
commands.description: A comparative analysis pattern used to detect logic drift between two different implementations of similar logic.
Adjust the number of code snippets retrieved from the local index to balance context relevance against token consumption.
